zh.lifeSummary: 當Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller於1681年9月27日出生在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧斯塔福特時，她的父親Hanß Nicolauß Müller24歲，母親Anna Barbara Stober31歲。她於1707年10月25日與Hanß Peter Stober在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧斯塔福特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1752年1月6日在她的家鄉享年70歲，埋葬在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧史額可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller于1681年9月27日出生在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧斯塔福特时，她的父亲Hanß Nicolauß Müller24岁，母亲Anna Barbara Stober31岁。她于1707年10月25日与Hanß Peter Stober在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧斯塔福特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1752年1月6日在她的家乡享年70岁，埋葬在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧史額可。

nameMeanings.name: Mueller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Gerhard, Klaus, Heinz, Helmut, Wolfgang, Manfred, Horst.

German (mainly Müller) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Müller); Dutch: occupational name for a miller, Middle High German müller, German Müller. In Germany, Müller, Mueller is the most frequent of all surnames; in the US it is often changed to Miller or appears as Muller . See also Mulder .

variants.name: Overmiller
meanings: Americanized form of German Obermüller, distinguishing name for a miller, Middle High German müller (see Mueller ) who worked the upper (Middle High German ober) mill.

variants.name: Miler
meanings: Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, Slovenian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a miller, from a Slavicized and Yiddishized form of German Müller (see Mueller ), often applied as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic occupational names or surnames. Compare Miller .

variants.name: Molitor
meanings: German, Walloon, and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): humanistic surname, a translation into Latin of German Mueller and French or Walloon Meunier , from Latin molitor ‘miller’.

variants.name: Mulder
meanings: Dutch: occupational name from mulder ‘miller’. This surname is also established in South Africa.
German (also Mülder): variant of Müller (see Mueller ), a cognate of 1 above.
German: occupational name for a maker of wooden bowls, from Middle High German mulde ‘bowl, trough, tub’ + the agent suffix -er.

variants.name: Miller
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a miller. The standard modern vocabulary word represents the northern Middle English term miller, an agent derivative of mille ‘mill’, reinforced by Old Norse mylnari (see Milner ). In southern, western, and central England Millward (literally, ‘mill keeper’) was the usual term. In North America, the surname Miller has absorbed many cognate surnames from other languages, for example German Müller (see Mueller ), Dutch Mulder and Molenaar , French Meunier , Italian Molinaro , Spanish Molinero , Hungarian Molnár (see Molnar ), Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Mlinar , Polish Młynarz or Młynarczyk (see Mlynarczyk ). Miller (including in the senses below) is the seventh most frequent surname in the US.

South German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Müller ‘miller’ (see Mueller ) and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in other European countries, notably in Poland, Denmark, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), and Czechia; compare 3 below.
Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovenian   Miler   ‘miller’, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Lockmiller
meanings: Americanized form of German Lochmüller, a distinguishing name for a miller (see Mueller ) whose mill was in a valley or hollow (Middle High German loch).

variants.name: Reinmiller
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Aloysius, Kurt.
Americanized form of German Reinmüller: distinguishing name for a miller (see Mueller ) with Rein 1 as the first element.

variants.name: Ostermiller
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gottlieb, Kurt.
Americanized form of German Ostermüller, a distinguishing name for a miller (see Mueller ) whose mill was situated to the east (see Oster ) of a settlement.

variants.name: Muller
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Franz, Gerhard, Uwe, Dieter, Heinz, Jurgen.
German (Müller) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; mainly Müller): see Mueller . This surname is also very common in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine).
Dutch: variant of   Mulder  .

variants.name: Muellner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Benno, Math, Mathias.
German (Müllner): variant of Müller (see Mueller ), from Middle High German mülner. Compare Mullner .

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: A name corrpted from Hannah, q.v. It is also found several times in a male name. From Ann, Anne, we have as nicknames Nan and Nancy

nameMeanings.name: Catharina
meanings: English: An English form of Catharine.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabetha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament. See Elizabeth .

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller was born on 27 September 1681, in Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, her father, Hanß Nicolauß Müller, was 24 and her mother, Anna Barbara Stober, was 31. She married Hanß Peter Stober on 25 October 1707, in Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 6 January 1752, in her hometown, at the age of 70, and was buried in Spöck, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller est née le 27 septembre 1681, à Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, son père, Hanß Nicolauß Müller, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Anna Barbara Stober, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Hanß Peter Stober le 25 octobre 1707, à Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 janvier 1752, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Spöck, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller am 27. September 1681 in Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hanß Nicolauß Müller 24 und ihre Mutter Anna Barbara Stober 31. Sie hat am 25. Oktober 1707 in Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden Hanß Peter Stober geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. Januar 1752 im Alter von 70 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Spöck, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller è nata il il 27 settembre 1681 nel Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, sua padre, Hanß Nicolauß Müller, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Anna Barbara Stober, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Hanß Peter Stober il 25 ottobre 1707, nel Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 6 gennaio 1752, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Spöck, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden.

ja.lifeSummary: Anna Catharina Elisabetha Muellerが1681年9月27日にバデンベズイルクサムト カルルスルヘスタフォートで生まれた時，父Hanß Nicolauß Müllerは24歳，母Anna Barbara Stoberは31歳であった。1707年10月25日にバデンベズイルクサムト カルルスルヘスタフォートでHanß Peter Stoberと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1752年1月6日に故郷で70歳で死去し，バデンベズイルクサムト カルルスルヘスプoックにて埋葬された。

ko.Spöck, Stutensee, Karlsruhe, Baden-Württ: 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 칼스루헤 스투텐시 스poc크
ko.lifeSummary: Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller(이)가 1681년 9월 27일 바든 베지르크샘트 칼스루헤 스태포트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hanß Nicolauß Müller(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna Barbara Stober(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1707년 10월 25일 바든 베지르크샘트 칼스루헤 스태포트에서 Hanß Peter Stober(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1752년 1월 6일 그녀의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 바든 베지르크샘트 칼스루헤 스poc크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller nasceu em 27 de setembro de 1681, em Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, seu pai, Hanß Nicolauß Müller, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Anna Barbara Stober, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Hanß Peter Stober em 25 de outubro de 1707, em Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 6 de janeiro de 1752, em sua cidade natal, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Spöck, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller родилась 27 сентября 1681 г. в местности стаффорт, безирксэмт карлсрух, баден, её отцу по имени Hanß Nicolauß Müller было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Anna Barbara Stober, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hanß Peter Stober 25 октября 1707 г. в местности стаффорт, безирксэмт карлсрух, баден. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 6 января 1752 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности спoкк, безирксэмт карлсрух, баден.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anna Catharina Elisabetha Mueller nació el 27 de septiembre de 1681, en Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, su papá, Hanß Nicolauß Müller, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Anna Barbara Stober, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Hanß Peter Stober el 25 de octubre de 1707, en Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 6 de enero de 1752, en su pueblo, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Spöck, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden.
